prompt_adapter_append: if shuttle launch hardware appears, keep Challenger as a faithful NASA Space Shuttle stack with correct orbiter, external tank, and twin solid rocket boosters
if the shuttle appears it must read as a winged shuttle orbiter attached to a rust-orange external tank flanked by two white solid rocket boosters, not a single cylindrical rocket
favor real launch-site and institutional context over abstract monolith collapse or generic rocket simplification
preserve the orbiter nose, delta-wing body, and booster relationship as recognizable shuttle geometry rather than simplifying them away
if any exhaust or flame appears, it must originate only from aft booster nozzles or orbiter main engines at the rear, never from the nose, sidewalls, mid-body, or forward joints
